S contains only lowercase letters.\n \nExample 1:\nInput: S="geeksforgeeks"\nOutput: e\nExplanation: \'e\' repeats at third position.\n \nExample 2:\nInput: S="hellogeeks"\nOutput: l\nExplanation: \'l\' repeats at fourth position.\n \nExample 3:\nInput: S="abc"\nOutput: -1\nExplanation: There is no repeated character.\nYour Task:  \nYou d' code='def first_repeated_character(s: str) -> str:\n    seen = set()\n    for char in s:\n        if char in seen:\n            return char\n        seen.add(char)\n    return "-1"'

Find if it is possible to choose exactly M elements from the array such that the MEX of the chosen elements is exactly K.\n\nRecall that the *MEX* of an array is the smallest non-negative integer that does not belong to the array. For example, the *MEX* of [2, 2, 1] is 0 because 0 does not belong to the array, the *MEX* of [3, 1, 0, 1] is 2 because 0 and 1 belong to the array, but 2 does not.\n\n------ Input Format ------ \n\n- The first line contains an' code='from collections import Counter\n\ndef can_achieve_mex(A, M, K):\n    # Count the occurrences of each element in the array\n    b = Counter(A)\n    \n    # Count the number of elements less than K\n    s_count = sum(1 for x in b if x < K)\n    \n    # Check the conditions for achieving the required MEX\n    if s_count < K or K > M or M > len(A) - b.get(K, 0):\n        return False\n    else:\n        return True'
